title: Kerala Prawn Moilee

subs.name: firm white fish steaks for prawn moilee → fish moilee
subs.notes: increase cooking time to 8–10 minutes in step 5

subs.name: fresh coconut ground and squeezed through a cloth
subs.notes: homemade coconut milk gives a more intense flavour

ingredients.name: thin coconut milk (second extract)
visual_checks: dilute first extract 1:1 with water if needed
ingredients.notes: dilute first extract 1:1 with water if needed

subs.name: fresh coconut ground and squeezed through a cloth
subs.notes: homemade coconut milk gives a more intense flavour

subs.name: a small piece of kokum soaked in 2 tbsp water
subs.notes: kokum adds a fruity sourness that works beautifully with prawn

steps.text: Toss the cleaned prawns with half the turmeric and a little salt. Set aside for 10 minutes.
Heat coconut oil in a wide pan on medium flame. Add cardamom, cloves and cinnamon and let them sizzle for 20 seconds. Add the sliced onion and cook for 4–5 minutes until soft and pale gold.
Add ginger, garlic and green chillies. Saute for 2 minutes until fragrant. Add remaining turmeric and cracked black pepper, stir for 30 seconds.
Pour in the thin coconut milk and add the tomato slices. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for 3 minutes. The gravy will turn a warm yellow.

Add the marinated prawns and curry leaves. Cook on medium flame for 4–5 minutes, turning the prawns once — they are done when they curl tightly and turn opaque pink. Do not overcook or they will toughen.
Reduce heat to the lowest setting. Pour in the thick coconut milk. Swirl the pan gently, heat for 2 minutes without boiling. Add lemon juice and check salt.
Remove from heat and serve immediately with Kerala appam, idiyappam or steamed rice. The gravy is delicate and should be eaten fresh.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Cook seafood until safely done for the species and preparation; keep it chilled before cooking.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: prawn → firm white fish steaks for prawn moilee → fish moilee (increase cooking time to 8–10 minutes in step 5); coconut-milk → fresh coconut ground and squeezed through a cloth (homemade coconut milk gives a more intense flavour); lemon → a small piece of kokum soaked in 2 tbsp water (kokum adds a fruity sourness that works beautifully with prawn)
